script:
Indian markets just had a brutal Black Wednesday. Trump scrapped the Iran ceasefire deal, sending crude oil surging nearly five percent. Nifty plunged over five hundred points, and India VIX spiked sharply — a level traders say was worse than the US-Iran war shock. Nearly eight lakh crore rupees were wiped out in a single session. The critical support zone around twenty-three thousand eight hundred is now under close watch ahead of Thursday's weekly expiry.

Meanwhile, the rupee crashed to an intraday low near ninety-five against the dollar before the RBI reportedly stepped in to defend the currency. Analysts are calling this a toxic macro cocktail — weak rupee plus high oil means a punishing import bill for India.

Before trading forex, always verify your broker on WIKIFX and check the RBI Alert List.
